results.rendered_html: śata—dru f. ‘flowing in a h˚ (or numerous) branches’, N. of a river now called the Sutlej (it is the most easterly of the five rivers of the Pañjāb, and rises in a lake [prob. Mānasa Sarovar] on the Himālaya mountains; flowing in a southwesterly direction for 550 miles, it unites with the Vipāśā or Beas south-east of Amritsar [see vipāś], afterwards joining the Chenāb and filling into the Indus below Multan; it is also called śutu-dri, śutu-dru, śita-dru &c.),  MBh. ;  Hariv. ;  R.  &c.
